business.headline: More than 3,600 trades in 90 days: Why Trump's portfolio is under scrutinyA financial disclosure showed US President Donald Trump's investment accounts made 3,642 trades in 90 days. The filing has prompted ethics questions, calls for an investigation and debate over conflicts of interest.

India has $700 billion in forex reserves. Why is foreign capital still a concern?India has built a formidable forex cushion. But in an increasingly unpredictable world, will that be enough if foreign money starts drying up?

Stock market opens higher for fourth day as Brent crude slips below $79The S&P BSE Sensex was up 179.03 points, or 0.23%, at 76,987.51 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 46.45 points, or 0.19%, to trade at 24,035.60. The Nifty once again remained above the psychologically important 24,000 mark.

Jio may file $4 billion IPO papers before Reliance AGMReliance Jio may file draft papers for a $4 billion IPO within days, possibly before Reliance Industries' Friday AGM. The move would revive focus on one of India's most anticipated listings after delays and a slowdown in the IPO market.

Pizza Hut sold for $2.7 billion: Pizza chain many grew up with gets a new ownerYum Brands has agreed to sell Pizza Hut for $2.7 billion, splitting China from the rest of the business. The deal gives Yum China greater control in a key market and offers LongRange Capital a chance to revive the global chain.

Gold rallies, oil prices retreat on optimism over US-Iran peace dealGold rose on Tuesday after an interim US-Iran deal eased pressure on oil prices. Lower inflation fears also reduced expectations of a Federal Reserve rate hike later this year.

Why India's wealthy are quietly buying second residencies abroadThey are not leaving India. But they want the option to, just in case. From Dubai to Portugal to Greece, promoters and business families are spending crores on second residencies abroad. It is not about emigration. It is about insurance.

8th Pay Commission: Memorandum deadline ends. What have unions asked forThe deadline for 8th Pay Commission proposals has ended, bringing employee and pensioner demands before the panel. The next phase will test which calls on salary, DA, fitment and pensions shape the final recommendations.

Sensex ends 544 points higher, Nifty nears 24,000; HUL up 2%The S&P BSE Sensex climbed 544.15 points, or 0.71%, to settle at 76,808.48, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 135.25 points, or 0.57%, to close at 23,989.15, just shy of the crucial 24,000 mark.

11 digital tricks your bank can no longer play on youThe RBI has named and banned 11 specific digital manipulation tactics that banks use on their apps and websites to push products you never asked for. Here is what each one means and how it was being used against you.

US-Iran deal eases oil, but India's petrol relief may take longerThe US-Iran deal has reopened the Strait of Hormuz and cooled crude prices. But India may not see immediate fuel relief as supply chains and inventories recover.

World's 1st trillionaire says a quadrillion-dollar fortune 'not impossible'Elon Musk said becoming the world's first quadrillionaire is "not impossible" and linked it to factories on the Moon and Mars. He also said dollars may no longer matter in a future shaped by AI, robots and interplanetary industry.

Japan raises interest rates to 31-year high as West Asia war fuels inflation fearsThe Bank of Japan raised its policy rate to 1% from 0.75%, the highest level since 1995, citing faster pass-through from crude oil prices. The move signalled concern that oil-led cost pressures could push inflation above target while keeping focus on gradual further hikes.

Dalal Street extends gains as Iran-US peace deal eases inflation concernsThe S&P BSE Sensex gained 306.22 points, or 0.40%, to 76,570.55 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 rose 72.95 points, or 0.31%, to 23,926.85 as of 9:36 am.

Finally, RBI takes your side, comes hard on banks for mis-sellingThe RBI has issued binding directions banning forced bundling, mis-selling and manipulative digital sales tactics by commercial banks. The rules introduce refunds, tighter consent norms and direct accountability for agents from January 1, 2027.

Govt hikes special duty on diesel, aviation fuel exports amid West Asia crisisThe Centre has increased the windfall profit tax on diesel exports to Rs 14 per litre and on ATF exports to Rs 12.5 per litre from June 16, while retaining the petrol export duty at Rs 1.5 per litre.

India-US trade deal enters final lap as fresh talks scheduled on June 23-24India and the United States will hold trade talks on June 23 and 24 to finalise the first tranche of their proposed bilateral trade agreement. The meeting could shape the pace of the wider pact as both sides work through market access and trade barriers.

The hidden intelligence gap in India's renewable energy transitionA utility-scale solar-plus-storage project in India was found to be losing Rs 20–25 lakh a month despite strong uptime. The case highlights why India's renewable build-out needs software-led grid and market optimisation.

Sensex ends 736 points higher, Nifty above 23,800; IndiGo up 4%The S&P BSE Sensex surged 736.38 points, or 0.97%, to settle at 76,264.33, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 231 points, or 0.98%, to close at 23,853.90. The rally was broad-based, with strong buying seen in auto, real estate, industrial and consumption-related stocks.

Why the US-Iran peace deal is good news for IndiaThe reported US-Iran agreement to end hostilities and reopen the Strait of Hormuz has eased immediate pressure on India's oil-dependent economy. Its broader benefit will depend on whether lower crude prices and calmer trade routes hold.

entertainment.headline: Manoj Bajpayee not the first choice for The Family Man, this Telugu superstar was Filmmaker Raj Nidimoru, in a recent interview, revealed that they had considered a Telugu superstar to play Manoj Bajpayee's role in The Family Man. Also, he explained that the show was initially conceived as a film.

Samantha scores pre-release box office win as Maa Inti Bangaram turns profitable Actor Samantha Ruth Prabhu's Maa Inti Bangaram has recovered its full investment before its June 19 release. The pre-release business was driven by firm demand for rights across territories and platforms.

Ravi Kishan reacts to actor Sanchita Ugale's suicide, advocates meditation Ravi Kishan reacted after actor Sanchita Ugale died by suicide in Mumbai at 26. He emphasised the significance of meditation, spirituality, and emotional strength to navigate life's hardships and urged everyone to keep hope alive.

New mom Kiara Advani makes a striking runway comeback, Karan Johar joins. Video Actor Kiara Advani and filmmaker Karan Johar stole the spotlight at Rahul Mishra's fashion show with a headline-making runway appearance. Here's what happened at the star-studded event and what Kiara has been up to lately.

Christopher Nolan to attend The Odyssey premiere in Mumbai Christopher Nolan is set to attend the Mumbai premiere of The Odyssey in July with Matt Damon, Tom Holland and Emma Thomas. The event makes Mumbai an official stop on the film's global tour and marks Nolan's first premiere in India.

Sonu Nigam undergoes treatment for painful nerve condition ahead of Mumbai concert Sonu Nigam shared that he is battling pinched nerves and has been undergoing medical tests and treatment for the past week. Despite the pain and its impact on his throat, the singer has assured fans that he will perform at his Mumbai concert on June 27.

Shrek 5 teaser trailer drops a mystery as trouble brews for the ogre family Shrek 5's first look teases unexpected trouble for Shrek's family, a mysterious city adventure and several unanswered questions about what lies ahead.

Composer GV Prakash donates Rs 50,000 for medical aid after X user's appeal Composer and actor GV Prakash Kumar donated Rs 50,000 after an X user sought urgent help for a friend's treatment at a Coimbatore hospital. The gesture drew praise online and also renewed discussion on verification in medical fundraisers.

Why Raakh is one of the most emotionally cathartic Indian shows in recent years Prime Video's Raakh revisits the 1978 Ranga-Billa case not as a crime thriller, but as a deeply human story of grief, loss and the lives left behind. It's an emotionally stirring watch that stays with you long after it ends.

Arbaaz Khan's security team drags man out of his car after shocking breach. Watch Arbaaz Khan's security team was seen pulling an unidentified man out of the actor's car after an alleged security breach during his Kolkata visit. Videos from the incident have gone viral on social media.

Actor-politician Ravi Kishan to participate with daughter Riva on Alliance India Bhojpuri superstar and politician Ravi Kishan will participate in Prime Video's new reality show Alliance India alongside his daughter Riva Kishan. The father-daughter duo joins the high-stakes competition hosted by Kunal Kemmu.

Samantha's supergirl era begins in a saree: The making of an action heroine Actor Samantha's journey from romantic roles to action performer now leads to Maa Inti Bangaram, where she plays a saree-clad housewife who fights back. The film extends a career shift shaped by The Family Man 2, Yashoda and Citadel: Honey Bunny.

Did Qubool Hai borrow from Pak drama Kabhi Main Kabhi Tum? Fans spot similarities Micro-drama Qubool Hai has landed in the middle of an online controversy after viewers drew comparisons with a beloved Pakistani drama Kabhi Main Kabhi Tum.

Disclosure Day: Why Steven Spielberg's new sci-fi film matters in today's world Steven Spielberg goes beyond serving sci-fi drama with his new film, Disclosure Day. He opens up a vital conversation. Watch the end closely, and you'll know.

The baby is having a baby: Smriti Irani meets Kyunki co-star Karishma Tannna Smriti Irani shared a heartfelt message for pregnant Karishma Tanna after their recent reunion, reflecting on the actor's journey from a young television newcomer to motherhood.

Sanchita Ugale's actor-friend says she spoke of wanting to die, battled depression Actor Sanchita Ugale's friend and actor Geetanjali has urged people not to speculate on her death. She also claimed Ugale battled depression and insomnia.

Insulting women: Fresh FIR against comedian Pranit More over Rs 370 biryani row In fresh trouble for comedian Pranit More and techie Himanshu Jangra, the Gurugram Police filed a case over the 'Rs 370 biryani' remark, normalising sexual coercion and misogyny.

I stand by it: Dia Mirza doubles down on 'patriarchy causes climate change' claim Dia Mirza has explained her remarks linking patriarchy to climate change after online backlash. She said her comments were about unequal power structures driving extraction and environmental harm.

Actor Rainn Wilson says The Office can't be made today, calls it politically wrong Rainn Wilson has said The Office would be difficult to make today because of its politically incorrect humour. He said the show's clueless and offensive characters drove the comedy.

general news.headline: Telegram challenges temporary blocking order in Delhi High Court, hearing todayThe platform has challenged the government's recent decision to impose a temporary ban on it in the run-up to the NEET UG re-test. The petition argues against the suspension of the messaging service and seeks relief from the court before the June 21 examination.

Live: Won't spare backstabbers, resign and go, Sanjay Raut dares rebel MPsShiv Sena UBT Crisis Live Updates: Amid speculation over a possible shift within Shiv Sena (UBT), sources clarified that no meeting took place between Shrikant Shinde and UBT MPs, contrary to earlier reports. Sources said that before any political move can proceed, the rebel group would first need to submit a formal letter to Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla. The clarification comes amid renewed political chatter over the stability of Uddhav Thackeray's parliamentary strength following reports of possible defections. Meanwhile, party leaders continue internal consultations and outreach, while maintaining that communication remains open within the organisation amid the ongoing political developments. Follow IndiaToday.in for the latest updates.

When India removed barricades from US Embassy and why the 2013 memory is back in 2026In 2013, the arrest and strip-search of an Indian diplomat in New York prompted a fierce and coordinated diplomatic retaliation from New Delhi. More than a decade later, three Indian sailors are dead, killed by the US Navy, and India's response has been markedly more restrained.
Meloni quits smoking, Trump gets a bike, Macron loses watch: G7 hot-mic momentsWhat world leaders said when they thought the microphones were off: G7 Summit hot-mic moments reveal candid exchanges.

Mithun Chakraborty: The unsung hero of BJP's West Bengal blockbuster showLong before the BJP won Bengal, Mithun Chakraborty had built a connection with audiences that politics alone could not create. In 2026, that old screen nostalgia turned into political recall as Bengal voted for change. Mithun da is one of the unsung heroes of the BJP's Bengal sweep.

IND A vs AFG A Live: India A look to rebuild after Sooryavanshi, Arya fallIndia (IND-A) vs Afghanistan (AFG-A) Live Score, Tri-Series 2026: India (151/3 in 22 ov). Ruturaj Gaikwad fell for 30 off 31 balls after Abdullah Ahmadzai dismissed him down the leg side. The Afghanistan pacer angled a full delivery towards the pads and Gaikwad's attempted glance resulted in a faint edge through to wicketkeeper Mohammad Ishaq, who completed a straightforward catch. His innings, which included four boundaries, ended just as he was looking to build on his start.zz

Finally, RBI takes your side, comes hard on banks for mis-sellingThe RBI has issued binding directions that ban forced bundling, mis-selling and manipulative digital sales tactics by commercial banks. From January 1, 2027, the rules will introduce mandatory refunds, tighter consent norms and direct accountability for agents.

Sena UBT MPs offered Rs 15 crore to switch, claims Sanjay Raut amid revolt rumoursA late-night claim of a Rs 15 crore offer to MPs has intensified speculation of a major rebellion within Shiv Sena UBT, prompting the party to issue a whip and scramble to contain the crisis.

The Great Exit Plan: Why Gen Z and Millennials are rewriting career goalsAs burnout, layoffs, and changing definitions of success reshape careers, many Gen Z and Millennials are building "exit plans" alongside their jobs. From side hustles to financial independence, the goal is no longer just career growth but freedom, flexibility, and the possibility of pursuing a more meaningful life.

Here's the evidence of Trump's defeat. The $300 billion bill from IranDonald Trump might call the potential peace deal with Iran a victory. But for much of the world, the deal's announcement by Trump doesn't reveal American dominance. Many see the deal as Washington negotiating its way out of a war it once promised would decisively reshape the Middle East.

US drops 'Indo' from Pacific Command name, reverts to original designationThe US has renamed the Indo-Pacific Command back to the Pacific Command. The move restores the command's historic identity while keeping its mission and area of responsibility unchanged.

Pizza Hut sold for $2.7 billion: Pizza chain many grew up with gets a new ownerYum Brands has agreed to sell Pizza Hut for $2.7 billion, splitting China from the rest of the business. The deal gives Yum China greater control in a key market and offers LongRange Capital a chance to revive the global chain.

Magical Messi sinks Algeria with hat-trick, equals all-time goal-scoring recordLionel Messi scored a historic hat trick as Argentina opened their FIFA World Cup title defence with a commanding 3-0 victory over Algeria in Kansas City on Tuesday, drawing level with Miroslav Klose as the tournament's all-time leading goalscorer.

Monsoon crisis: Clouds remain absent, rain deficit hits 40% across IndiaSatellite images and IMD data show the southwest monsoon has stalled over large parts of India. The widening rain deficit is raising concerns for kharif sowing and soil moisture.

India has $700 billion in forex reserves. Why is foreign capital still a concern?India has built a formidable forex cushion. But in an increasingly unpredictable world, will that be enough if foreign money starts drying up?

Eknath Shinde's meeting with rebel Uddhav Sena MPs called off amid defection buzz Live UpdatesFresh split fears have surfaced in the Uddhav Sena, with six MPs reportedly in touch with the Eknath Shinde camp. A planned Delhi meeting with Shinde was called off at the last minute, while Sanjay Raut rejected defection claims and accused the BJP of using pressure tactics to engineer a split.
Trump's Golden Hour is 24 Carat Humiliation for AmericaTrump wanted unconditional surrender from Iran, and he said so loudly. An unconditional surrender did happen. His.

$500 million in value wiped out in a day: Turkey's Celebi on India ousterMore than a year after being forced out of Indian airports in the wake of Turkey's support to Pakistan during Operation Sindoor, Turkish aviation services firm Celebi Aviation says India's revocation of the security clearance granted to its Indian arm wiped out up to $500 million in value overnight.

JDS moves its MLAs to resort amid cross-voting fears before tomorrow's MLC pollsJanata Dal (Secular) will shift their MLAs to a resort on the outskirts of Bengaluru near Devanahalli later in the evening, with senior party leaders likely to hold briefings and sessions for during the legislators' stay.

Several Indians lost their lives: PM flags sailors' safety at G7 in Trump's presenceAddressing the Outreach Session at the G7 Summit in France, PM Modi said the conflict had inflicted significant human and economic costs on countries across the West Asia.

Not just sarees: Kangana Ranaut's love letter to Indian textilesDuring the promotions of her film Bharat Bhhagya Viddhaata, Kangana Ranaut wore sarees representing varied craft traditions from several states.

PM Modi invites UAE President to BRICS Summit during G7 meetingPrime Minister Narendra Modi invited UAE President Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan to attend the BRICS Summit during their meeting on the sidelines of the G7 Summit in France. The two leaders also reviewed bilateral cooperation and stressed dialogue, international law and safe navigation through the Strait of Hormuz.

Telegram banned to stop NEET leaks, but will it? 5 questions that need answersIndia's Telegram ban has triggered a wave of discussions online – as these things always do – regarding censorship and if at all, steps like these, can curb paper leaks.

Leave me: Video shows IAF personnel's wife pleading in alleged conversion, rape caseAccording to the complaint, the woman's former classmate lured her to a hotel on the pretext of a property deal, drugged and raped her, recorded obscene videos, and later used them to blackmail and sexually exploit her. She also alleged being extorted of Rs 3.09 lakh and pressured to convert to Islam.

sports.headline: Should Lionel Messi have seen red vs Algeria? Fans debate decision FIFA World Cup 2026: Lionel Messi hit a hat-trick as Argentina beat Algeria 3-0 in their World Cup opener. A disputed challenge on Aissa Mandi, ignored by the referee and VAR, kept the noise alive.

After hat-trick heroics, Messi reveals why he cried on record World Cup night Lionel Messi said he had gone through "difficult days" away from football before his record-equalling hat-trick against Algeria, explaining the emotional scenes that followed his opening goal in Argentina's World Cup opener.

IND A vs AFG A Live: Sooryavanshi, Arya, Ruturaj fall after India A's quick start India (IND-A) vs Afghanistan (AFG-A) Live Score, Tri-Series 2026: India (137/3 in 20 ov). Ruturaj Gaikwad fell for 30 off 31 balls after Abdullah Ahmadzai dismissed him down the leg side. The Afghanistan pacer angled a full delivery towards the pads and Gaikwad's attempted glance resulted in a faint edge through to wicketkeeper Mohammad Ishaq, who completed a straightforward catch. His innings, which included four boundaries, ended just as he was looking to build on his start.zz

Magical Messi sinks Algeria with hat-trick, equals all-time goal-scoring record Lionel Messi scored a historic hat trick as Argentina opened their FIFA World Cup title defence with a commanding 3-0 victory over Algeria in Kansas City on Tuesday, drawing level with Miroslav Klose as the tournament's all-time leading goalscorer.

Hungry like a debutant, Rohit Sharma targets big statement in Lucknow ODI India lead Afghanistan 1-0. The 2nd ODI at Lucknow's Ekana Stadium today is a chance to seal the series, but one subplot will shadow everything else from the first ball.

From Al Qaeda killing his father to scoring at World Cup, meet Iraq's captain fantastic FIFA World Cup 2026: Aymen Hussein scored Iraq's lone goal in a 4-1 World Cup defeat to Norway after a journey marked by war and personal loss. His equaliser, days after a seven-hour detention in Chicago, underlined why he stands as a national symbol of resilience in Iraq.

Lionel Messi scores hat-trick, equals Miroslav Klose's all-time World Cup goals record Lionel Messi scored a hat-trick to take his World Cup tally to 16 goals, drawing level with German legend Miroslav Klose at the top of the tournament's all-time scoring charts.

Kylian Mbappe vows to make history after record-breaking night for France FIFA World Cup 2026: Kylian Mbappe scored twice as France beat Senegal 3-1 in New Jersey and set a new national scoring record. He said the milestone matters less than helping France improve and chase another World Cup.

Thomas Partey to miss Ghana World Cup opener in Canada after visa appeal rejected Ghana midfielder Thomas Partey will miss his country's opening match of the 2026 FIFA World Cup after a Canadian federal court rejected an appeal seeking to overturn a visa refusal that has prevented him from entering the country.

Months after Manchester United sack, Ruben Amorim lands AC Milan job AC Milan have appointed Ruben Amorim as head coach after dismissing Massimiliano Allegri. Amorim takes up a managerial role for the first time since being sacked by Manchester United.

Shafali Verma in focus as India Women look for more fine-tuning vs Netherlands Women's T20 World Cup, IND vs NED: Harmanpreet Kaur's India have a few areas to address ahead of their clash against the Netherlands, before tougher challenges against South Africa and Australia later in the tournament.

Erling Haaland announces World Cup arrival with brace as Norway beat Iraq 4-1Erling Haaland struck twice as Norway opened their World Cup return with a 4-1 win over Iraq. The scoreline put Norway top of Group I, despite not having the perfect game in Boston.

AUS 2 vs 1 JOR Live: Jordan equalises with a screamer of their ownArgentina (ARG) vs Algeria (ALG) Live score, FIFA World Cup 2026: After Lionel Messi scored his first World Cup hat-trick to light up Group J, Austria makes their first World Cup appearance since 1998 to take on Jordan in California. Both teams have scored a screamer to keep the score level.

Portugal downplay Roberto Martinez exit talk before World Cup openerFIFA World Cup 2026: Portugal have insisted Roberto Martinez and the federation are focused only on the World Cup. The statement comes amid reports over his contract and growing speculation about his exit from the managerial role after the World Cup.

Mbappe's record-breaking brace powers France to 3-1 win vs SenegalFIFA World Cup 2026: France overcame a spirited Senegal challenge to begin their FIFA World Cup 2026 campaign with a 3-1 victory. Kylian Mbappe scored twice, broke Olivier Giroud's long-standing scoring record and delivered another reminder of his remarkable World Cup pedigree.

Neymar boost for Brazil: Superstar begins training ahead of Haiti World Cup clashNeymar has resumed individual training with Brazil after his right calf injury. The development lifts Brazil before the Haiti game, as they look to bounce back from their draw to Morocco.

Virat Kohli meets Jordan Cox in London before England Test debutVirat Kohli was seen with RCB team-mate Jordan Cox in London on the eve of the England batter's Test debut. The meeting drew attention as Cox prepares for a long-awaited chance at The Oval and Kohli's appearances in England continue to create buzz.

Snake trouble at World Cup 2026: Germany training base gets unexpected guestGermany captain Joshua Kimmich said a copperhead snake was spotted at the team's Winston-Salem training base. The incident has unsettled players and underlined an unusual off-field hazard for teams based across the United States.

IRQ vs NOR Highlights: Haaland scores brace in Norway 4-1 winIraq (IRQ) vs Norway (NOR) Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: Erling Haaland announced his arrival on the World Cup stage in emphatic fashion, scoring twice as Norway brushed aside Iraq 4-1 in Boston. The Manchester City striker fell short of a hat-trick, but still left with the match ball headlines firmly belonging to him.

New Zealand in danger of early exit as Sri Lanka stun defending championsWomen's T20 World Cup: Sri Lanka stunned defending champions New Zealand, winning by five wickets on Tuesday in Southampton. Nilakshika Silva's unbeaten half-century took Sri Lanka past the finish line.

Hashmaullah Shahidi urges Afghanistan to take ownership and make India grindIND vs AFG, 2nd ODI: Hashmatullah Shahidi said Afghanistan need to take greater responsibility and learn how to sustain pressure on India when the teams meet in the second ODI on Wednesday.

Reporters mock Son Heung-min's military service. South Korea players boycott pressSouth Korea's players refused media interactions after leaked remarks mocking Son Heung-min's military status surfaced. The episode prompted a sharp KFA response and deepened tensions with sections of the domestic press.

Messi 200: Champions Argentina ready for landmark World Cup opener vs AlgeriaLionel Messi will walk out for his 200th match in the Argentina shirt when the defending champions begin their FIFA World Cup 2026 campaign against Algeria. With Argentina rallying around their captain and Algeria arriving as dangerous underdogs, the world champions begin their title defence in Kansas City.

Richa Ghosh wary of Netherlands bowlers, India not willing to undermine debutantsWomen's T20 World Cup 2026, IND vs NED: Speaking to reporters ahead of Wednesday's match, Richa Ghosh said India would not take the Netherlands lightly despite them being tournament debutants, noting that they possess a strong bowling attack.

0.1 percent chance: Root digs old blazer from garage for Test captaincy returnEngland vs New Zealand, 2nd Test: Joe Root never expected to captain England again, but a week of turmoil has forced the former skipper back into the role after dusting off a creased blazer from his garage and stepping into the spotlight once more.

Richa Ghosh credits Harmanpreet, Smriti for helping her overcome lean patchRicha Ghosh credited Smriti Mandhana, Harmanpreet Kaur, and head coach Amol Muzumdar for backing her through a lean patch, saying their advice helped her stay positive and trust the process.

Action against Sooryavanshi? BCCI, Team India play down Dambulla controversyWhile BCCI Secretary Devajit Saikia has ruled out disciplinary action against Vaibhav Sooryavanshi for his post-match outburst in Dambulla, senior India coach Sairaj Bahutule has hit back at the Sri Lanka A players for provoking the 15-year-old prodigy.

Pakistan penalised for slow over rate after humiliating defeat vs IndiaPakistan's disappointing Women's T20 World Cup campaign suffered another setback after the ICC fined the team five per cent of their match fee for a slow over-rate during their 64-run defeat to India at Edgbaston.

Blow for Thomas Tuchel's England as Tino Livramento gamble backfiresFIFA World Cup 2026: England defender Tino Livramento has reportedly been ruled out before their opener after a hamstring injury. England begin their campaign on Wednesday in a blockbuster Group L game against Croatia.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
